description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who must reduce storage costs by archiving last year’s detailed monthly cloud-cost reports while keeping a lightweight summary online.

    Current state
    -------------
    1. Directory /home/user/cloud_reports/ already exists and contains 14 files:
       • cost_report_2023-01.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-02.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-03.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-04.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-05.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-06.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-07.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-08.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-09.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-10.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-11.csv  
       • cost_report_2023-12.csv  
       • summary_2023.csv  
       • README.txt (leave this file untouched)

       Each cost_report_2023-XX.csv file is a tiny two-line CSV:
          Month,TotalUSD
          2023-XX,1000

       summary_2023.csv is a single-row CSV:
          Year,TotalUSD
          2023,12000

    Goal
    ----
    1. Make a compressed archive of ONLY the 12 detailed cost_report_2023-XX.csv files.  
       • Archive path: /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_backup_20240315.tar.gz  
       • Compression: gzip (i.e. tar -czf …)

    2. Produce a manifest log file in /home/user/backup named cloud_reports_2023_manifest.log that contains SHA-256 hashes of every file placed in the archive.

       Exact manifest format (must match character-for-character):
       • First line: “# Manifest for 2023 cost reports backup generated on 2024-03-15”  
       • Subsequent 12 lines: one for each CSV included in the archive, sorted alphabetically by filename, with two spaces separating the hash and the filename.
         Example line pattern:
             d2d2d0bfbc41daa9d03bf1e9f7bd7d417be99f3787b4bbec9e14a626ea2f0e30  cost_report_2023-01.csv
       (Two spaces between hash and filename, newline at the end of every line.)

    3. After creating the archive and manifest, remove the original cost_report_2023-XX.csv files from /home/user/cloud_reports so the directory now only contains:
       • summary_2023.csv  
       • README.txt

    4. Leave all other files and directories untouched.

    What to deliver
    ---------------
    A. Archive file: /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_backup_20240315.tar.gz  
    B. Manifest file: /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_manifest.log  
    C. /home/user/cloud_reports/ directory should list exactly two files: summary_2023.csv and README.txt

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    • The automated test will extract the archive, compute SHA-256 hashes of its contents, compare them to the values found in cloud_reports_2023_manifest.log and expect an exact match (content and alphabetical order).  
    • The test will confirm that the 12 detailed CSV files no longer exist in /home/user/cloud_reports/.  
    • The manifest header line and archive/manifest file names must match precisely the strings given above; do NOT substitute today’s real date.

    Hint: You’ll need roughly 5-10 shell commands. Common tools include mkdir, tar, sha256sum, sort, and rm. Make sure /home/user/backup/ exists before writing files there.
truth:
Pre-existing structure and contents before the task starts:

    Directory /home/user/cloud_reports/ :
        cost_report_2023-01.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-01,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-02.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-02,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-03.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-03,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-04.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-04,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-05.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-05,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-06.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-06,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-07.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-07,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-08.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-08,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-09.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-09,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-10.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-10,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-11.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-11,1000\n")
        cost_report_2023-12.csv  (content exactly: "Month,TotalUSD\n2023-12,1000\n")
        summary_2023.csv         (content exactly: "Year,TotalUSD\n2023,12000\n")
        README.txt               (content arbitrary; keep intact)

    Directory /home/user/backup/ does NOT exist initially and must be created by the agent.

    Expected files after successful task:

        1. /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_backup_20240315.tar.gz
             – Contains the 12 cost_report_2023-XX.csv files with their original paths stripped (stored in archive root).
        2. /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_manifest.log
             – Header line:
                   # Manifest for 2023 cost reports backup generated on 2024-03-15
             – 12 lines following, hashes generated via “sha256sum cost_report_2023-??.csv”, sorted alphabetically.

        3. /home/user/cloud_reports/ directory now only contains:
             summary_2023.csv  (unaltered)
             README.txt        (unaltered)

    The automated tests will:
        a) Assert that /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_backup_20240315.tar.gz exists.
        b) Extract that archive to a temp dir, producing the 12 CSVs.
        c) Compute SHA-256 hashes of those 12 files and compare—line by line—to /home/user/backup/cloud_reports_2023_manifest.log (exact two-space delimiter).
        d) Confirm manifest header line is exactly "# Manifest for 2023 cost reports backup generated on 2024-03-15".
        e) Verify /home/user/cloud_reports/ no longer contains any cost_report_2023-*.csv files and still contains summary_2023.csv and README.txt only.
